title: Argo CD ApplicationSet Controller Drift — Generated Applications vs Cluster Reality
dataSources: `index=devops` `sourcetype=argocd:applicationset` (controller reconcile events, status.conditions), optional `sourcetype=argocd:application` for join
app: Splunk Add-on for Argo CD, Argo CD ApplicationSet controller logs to HEC
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="argocd:applicationset"
| eval gen_count=mvcount(mvdedup(split(mvjoin(generated_apps, ","), ",")))
| eval ready=if(match(lower(status_phase),"(?i)healthy|synced|successful"),1,0)
| where ready=0 OR match(lower(message),"(?i)error|failed|unable to generate")
| stats latest(_time) as last_event, values(status_phase) as phases, latest(message) as msg, max(gen_count) as apps_claimed by applicationset_name, namespace, cluster
| sort -last_event
description: ApplicationSet is the factory for Argo CD Applications; when the controller cannot materialize expected apps or reports degraded conditions, new environments and tenants silently stop receiving GitOps coverage. Tracking reconcile errors and generated-app cardinality catches template mistakes, generator misconfigurations, and permission regressions before clusters run understaffed.
value: Prevents missing Application resources that would leave namespaces without sync policies, which otherwise surface only as unexplained deployment stalls or manual hotfixes.
implementation: Enable structured logging or HEC events from the ApplicationSet controller with fields such as applicationset_name, generated_apps, status_phase, and message. Normalize multi-value app lists. Schedule hourly; join to argocd:application for orphan detection if an ApplicationSet claims N apps but fewer exist with matching labels.
visualization: Single-value (open ApplicationSet faults), timechart of error rate by cluster, table (ApplicationSet × phase × message).
grandmaExplanation: We watch GitOps sync and Argo signals so clusters stay aligned with the desired configuration in Git.
knownFalsePositives: Drift from manual console fixes during incidents, automated tool changes, or planned config updates outside IaC.
